title: Question: plans / timeline for HTMX 4 support?
author: tbnorth
body:
### Description

I know HTMX 4 is beta but curious if you're considering making the HTMX version selectable in django_htmx?

title: Testing utilities for htmx views
author: earthcomfy
body:
### Description

Is there a plan to add testing support? Instead of rolling a boilerplate to simulate htmx requests and assert response headers, I'm thinking something like an HtmxClient (a django.test.Client subclass with convenience methods for making htmx requests without manual header construction) and assertion helpers.

Happy to send a PR if this direction seems reasonable.

title: Middleware with correct headers for htmx requests
author: jlucasp25
body:
### Description

In order to avoid caching errors, the `Vary` header should be set to `HX-Request ` as stated on the docs.

I've found common to implement on most HTMX projects a middleware that adds the following header if the request is made by HTMX so that I don't need to worry with setting it per view upfront.

My suggestion would be to add a middleware specific for that purpose.
If needed, I can help with the implementation.

Thanks! Keep up the good work!

title: Vendor extensions?
author: adamchainz
body:
### Description

#513 vendored htmx but still leaves users needing to download and manage extensions, when many are also slow-moving. Providing vendored versions of at least popular nad stable extensions, like the SSE one, may be an easy win for usability. Users could pull them into an htmx bundle with an extra argument to `{% htmx_script %}`, like `{% htmx_script with_extensions=sse %}`. 

We'd possibly want to cache the combined script file to avoid re-concatenating on every request.

title: Validating test client
author: adamchainz
body:
### Description

Testing htmx integration is hard. One idea to help here is a subclass of Django's [`Client`](https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/stable/topics/testing/tools/#django.test.Client) that detects HTML responses, parses them, and checks any htmx attributes found on nodes.

Example checks:

1. For `hx-get`, `hx-post`, and so on: if the given URL resolves with Django's URL resolver (and the request's URLconf, given it may have been swapped).
2. `hx-trigger`: potentially some custom parsing
3. For all attributes: typoed names, like `hx-gte` instead of `hx-get`.

I'm sure there are many more we could come up with.

The biggest risk in this idea is poor performance. It's almost a given that this approach will be fairly slow in Python, because it will require trawling the whole DOM for every test, when htmx attributes may only be used in some places.

title: `reselect()` HTTP function to set `hx-reselect`
author: adamchainz
body:
### Description

[`hx-reselect`](https://htmx.org/docs/#response-headers:~:text=HX%2DReselect) allows changing `hx-select` for a given response, added in https://github.com/bigskysoftware/htmx/commit/3521c3c059665d2833aed13d2a258b43dfd0b836.

We have `reswap()` for `hx-swap` and `retarget()` for `hx-retarget`. By analogy, we should have `reselect()`, even though its implementation will be very basic, like `retarget()`.

title: Update template partials example and docs for Django 6.0
author: adamchainz
body:
### Description

The existing example and docs walk through using django-template-partials. Since its functionality has been merged into the upcoming Django 6.0 (https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/6.0/releases/6.0/#template-partials), we should:

1. Update the example project to Django 6.0 and use the built-in support there.
2. Update the docs to cover how to do it on Django 6.0, now mentioning django-template-partials as a backport.

title: TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'innerText') on HTMX error responses
author: shasafoster

title: Automatically add CSP Nonce to script tags
author: anywaifu
body:
### Description

{% htmx-script %} doesn't allow to receive CSP_NONCE. My workaround was using like ` <script src="{% static 'django_htmx/htmx.min.js' %}" nonce="{{ CSP_NONCE }}" defer></script>` but then I don't have the optimization when debugging :/

title: js handling
author: jacopsd
body:
### Description

Most pages have dedicated js script, some with <script src=""> tags, some with inline js code.
django-htmx could load en execute these after an htmx fragment got loaded.
Ideally this should happen completely transparent, so old style pages can be migrated to htmx partial pages easily.

title: Handle 400 and 403 responses in extension script
author: adamchainz
body:
### Description

Django can show error pages for these too, best to show them full screen like 404s.

title: Reusable HTMX View Utilities for Django
author: cnr91
body:
### Python Version

_No response_

### Django Version

_No response_

### Package Version

_No response_

### Description

Instead of repeating the same HTMX request checks, toast message logic, modal controls, or event triggering in every view, I use this helper + decorator structure. Curious if others use similar structures, and open to suggestions!


### What common patterns does this avoid?
1. Rewriting return render(...) or JsonResponse(...) repeatedly
2. Handling HTMX-only views with manual checks like if not request.htmx:
3. Rewriting trigger_client_event(...) calls for common cases
4. Manually attaching event payloads for modals, refreshes, or toast messages
5. Spreading conditional response logic (template vs json vs raw HttpResponse)
6. Forgetting to wrap client events inside afterSwap, afterSettle or similar hooks
7. Duplicating frontend logic to close modals or refresh containers

 Decorator: require_htmx
```
from functools import wraps
from django.http import HttpResponse

def require_htmx(view_func):
    """
    Decorator that ensures the view is only accessible via HTMX requests.

    Args:
        view_func: The view function to be decorated.

    Returns:
        HttpResponse: Returns 400 if not an HTMX request, otherwise calls the view.
    """
    @wraps(view_func)
    def wrapped(request, *args, **kwargs):
        if not request.htmx:
            return HttpResponse("This endpoint is only available via HTMX requests.", status=400)
        return view_func(request, *args, **kwargs)
    return wrapped
```

Utility Function: htmx_response
```from django.http import JsonResponse, HttpResponse
from django.shortcuts import render

def htmx_response(
    *,
    request=None,
    template_name=None,
    context=None,
    json_data=None,
    response=None,
    message=None,
    message_type="success",
    status=None,
    events=None,          # Events triggered immediately via trigger_client_event
    timed_events=None,    # {"afterSwap": [...], "afterSettle": [...]}
    refresh_target=None,  # CSS selector(s) to trigger UI refresh
    close_modal=False     # Shortcut to close an open modal
) -> HttpResponse:
    """
    Versatile response builder for HTMX-based views. Supports:
        - Template rendering (with template_name and context)
        - JSON responses (via json_data)
        - Predefined HttpResponse (via response)
        - Client-side toast messages (message + message_type)
        - Immediate HTMX client events (via events)
        - Timed HTMX events (afterSwap, afterSettle, etc.)
        - UI refresh targeting (refresh_target as CSS selector)
        - Modal closing trigger (close_modal=True)

    Parameters:
        request (HttpRequest): Required if using template rendering.
        template_name (str): Template to render.
        context (dict): Context passed to the template.
        json_data (dict): JSON content to respond with.
        response (HttpResponse): If given, returns this directly.
        message (str): Message to show on the frontend.
        message_type (str): One of 'success', 'error', 'info', etc.
        status (int): HTTP status code (default 200).
        events (list): List of tuples → (event_name, payload_dict)
        timed_events (dict): Events keyed by HTMX timing ('afterSwap', etc.)
        refresh_target (str | list): CSS selector(s) to trigger frontend update.
        close_modal (bool): Shortcut to trigger modal close event.

    Example usage:

    # Simple template response
    return htmx_response(
        template_name="partials/user_list.html",
        context={"users": users}
    )

    # Message and client event
    return htmx_response(
        template_name="partials/product_card.html",
        context={"product": product},
        message="Product added to cart",
        message_type="success",
        events=[("updateCart", {"count": cart.count})]
    )

    # Timed events example
    return htmx_response(
        template_name="partials/form_result.html",
        context={"success": True},
        timed_events={
            "afterSwap": [("modal:show", {"target": "#successModal"})],
            "afterSettle": [("confetti:start", {})]
        }
    )

    # JSON response
    return htmx_response(
        json_data={"status": "ok", "data": serialized_data}
    )
    """
    status = status or 200
    events = events or []
    timed_events = timed_events or {}

    if response is not None:
        final_response = response
    elif json_data is not None:
        final_response = JsonResponse(json_data, status=status)
    elif template_name:
        if not request:
            raise ValueError("`request` must be provided when using `template_name`.")
        final_response = render(request, template_name, context or {}, status=status)
    else:
        final_response = HttpResponse(status=status)

    if message:
        events.append(("htmxMessage", {"type": message_type, "message": message}))

    if close_modal:
        events.append(("modalClose", {}))

    if refresh_target:
        if isinstance(refresh_target, str):
            refresh_target = [refresh_target]
        for idx, selector in enumerate(refresh_target):
            event_name = "refreshElement" if idx == 0 else f"refreshElement{idx+1}"
            events.append((event_name, {"target": selector}))

    for name, params in events:
        trigger_client_event(final_response, name, params or {})

    for timing, event_list in timed_events.items():
        for name, params in event_list:
            trigger_client_event(final_response, name, params or {}, after=timing)

    return final_response
```

### Usage Examples for htmx_response

1. Render a Partial Template
```
return htmx_response(
    request=request,
    template_name="partials/user_list.html",
    context={"users": users}
)
```


2. Return a JSON Response
```
return htmx_response(
    json_data={"status": "ok", "data": serialized_data}
)
```

3. Trigger Toast Message on Client
```
return htmx_response(
    request=request,
    template_name="partials/product_card.html",
    context={"product": product},
    message="Product added to cart",
    message_type="success"
)
```

4. Trigger Immediate Client Events
```
return htmx_response(
    request=request,
    template_name="partials/order_summary.html",
    context={"order": order},
    events=[
        ("updateCart", {"count": cart.count}),
        ("trackOrder", {"orderId": order.id})
    ]
)
```

5. Trigger Timed HTMX Events (afterSwap, afterSettle)
```
return htmx_response(
    request=request,
    template_name="partials/form_result.html",
    context={"success": True},
    timed_events={
        "afterSwap": [("modal:show", {"target": "#successModal"})],
        "afterSettle": [("confetti:start", {})]
    }
)
```

6. Refresh Specific UI Elements (by CSS Selector)
```
return htmx_response(
    request=request,
    template_name="partials/order_item.html",
    context={"item": item},
    refresh_target=["#cart-total", "#cart-items"]
)
```

7. Close Modal Automatically
```
return htmx_response(
    request=request,
    message="Changes saved successfully.",
    close_modal=True
)
```

8. Use Existing HttpResponse Directly
```
my_response = HttpResponse("Hello HTMX World", status=200)
return htmx_response(response=my_response)
```

title: Test `HttpResponseClientRedirect` with `preserve_request`

New attribute form 5.2 that changes the status code, try it out and see if anything needs changing. https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/5.2/ref/request-response/#django.http.HttpResponseRedirect

title: HtmxView
author: Alan-Manning

title: HttpResponseLocation affected by bug in HTMX 2.0.3
author: jhh
body:
### Python Version

3.12.7

### Django Version

5.1.3

### Package Version

1.21.0

### Description

Submitting this issue to track a bug in HTMX 2.0.3 that others might see after upgrading HTMX. The HTMX client will not submit a redirected request when using `HttpResponseLocation`.   See bigskysoftware/htmx#2958.

The fix appears to have been merged and should be in the 2.0.4 HTMX release.

title: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'django_htmx'
author: dgchrt
body:
### Python Version

3.10.5

### Django Version

5.0.7

### Package Version

_No response_

### Description

Running the `example` code causes a:
```sh
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'django_htmx'
```

It runs fine after manually installing the missing dependency via:
```sh
pip install django-htmx
```

I believe `django-htmx` is missing in the requirements files, so that the example can run out of the box.

title: hyperlink w/target="_blank" and hx-boost="false" is being reported as a boosted link
author: bschollnick
body:
### Python Version 3.12.5 ### Django Version 5.1.1 ### Package Version 1.19.0 ### Description I'm attempting to open the next page in a new window/tab, using target="_blank" in the hyperlink, e.g.:

{{ icon('fas fa-external-link-alt fa-3x') }}

My view logic is based entirely around the request.htmx.boost boolean: ``` if request.htmx.boosted: print("partial") template_name = "frontend/gallery_htmx_partial.jinja" else: print("full") template_name = "frontend/gallery_htmx_complete.jinja" if not filetypes.models.FILETYPE_DATA: print("Loading filetypes") filetypes.models.FILETYPE_DATA = filetypes.models.load_filetypes() i_uuid = str(i_uuid).strip().replace("/", "") context = build_context_info(request, i_uuid) | {"user": request.user} # | {"sort": sort_order(request), "uuid": i_uuid, } response = render( request, template_name, context, using="Jinja2" ) return response ``` This link is being shown as boosted, even though hx-boost is set false. I'm unclear if this is a htmx issue, or a django-htmx issue. How can I troubleshoot this further, to help identify what is causing this issue?

title: Django Test Client with HX-Request header
author: jlucasp25
body:
### Description

Hey, thanks for all the work.
It would be useful to have a pytest fixture/django test client with HTMX headers for testing requests.
Let me know what you think, I might help with the feature if needed.

title: 'select' kwarg support in HttpResponseLocation
author: Nizzy94
body:
### Python Version

3.12

### Django Version

5.0.2

### Package Version

1.18.0

### Description

I read the issue #461 and it was closed because the problem was claimed to be resolved. It hasn't been solved for me.
I was using version 1.17.2 when I realised the select kwarg was not supported. so I upgraded to 1.18.0, which in the documentation and source code shows the select kwag as part of the options.

when I hover over the one in my code, it doesn't show the select options, causing my code to fail. Without it, I would have to restructure a lot of code for it to work. below is a screenshot of the hover:
